title: Intensely Smoky Polish Pork Kebabs
description: A smoky and crispy Polish dish featuring rose water, perfect for crowds, this keto-friendly recipe is sure to please. With a focus on smoky flavors and a hint of rose water, these kebabs are a unique twist on traditional Polish cuisine.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Add the pork cubes to the bowl and toss to coat evenly with the spice mixture.
In a small bowl, whisk together rose water and olive oil, then pour over the pork cubes and toss to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight to marinate.
Preheat a grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit, using wood chips to generate smoke.
Thread the marinated pork cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each cube.
Grill the kebabs for 10-12 minutes, turning occasionally, until the pork is cooked through and slightly charred.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or rosemary if desired.
